head.hi: The Secretary of State to the Consul at Geneva (Gilbert)

div.p:
157. For Reber. Department’s 150, October 7, 5 p.m. and your 291, October 8, 6 p.m.Latter not printed. The following telegram has just been received from the Finance Corporation of America:
“Referring to your letter of October 5Department of State, Press Releases, October 15, 1932, p. 239. with enclosures of general principles and communication from Viscount Cecil to the American representative.
“The general principles are receiving our careful study and although we are not satisfied that the general principles are as yet in form to afford a solution, we are willing to send a representative to Geneva to explore in negotiation the possibility of agreement on a plan mutually acceptable and that will be of practical benefit to Liberia and her people.”

The Department is informed that the corporation will not have completed its studies or be in a position to send an instructed representative to commence negotiations before Midnovember. You may so inform the Committee.
